step_by_step_thinking: I agree with agent1's assessment. The primary site of lesion in RHD-related endocarditis indeed occurs along the lines of closure of the valves because these areas experience higher pressures and greater turbulence, promoting the attachment and growth of vegetations. Additionally, this region receives continuous impact from blood cells and debris, further increasing the likelihood of thrombus formation and subsequent inflammatory responses.
